title: Poa sanchez-vegae
intro: Poa sanchez-vegae, also known as Sanz-vega grass or mountain grass, is a species of grass native to the high mountains of the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including gastrointestinal issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Poa sanchez-vegae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In herbal medicine, Poa sanchez-vegae is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used as a component in some commercial herbal supplements and tinctures.
taxonomy: Poa sanchez-vegae is a species of grass, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Poaceae, subfamily Pooideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Poeae. Within this tribe, it is placed in the subtribe Poinae. Poa sanchez-vegae is also a part of the genus Poa. The classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
